name: Miniature Bull Terrier
description: The Miniature Bull Terrier is essentially a scaled-down version of the Bull Terrier, sharing the same distinctive egg-shaped head and exuberant, clownish personality in a more compact package. These robust little dogs are fearless, full of energy, and deeply attached to their families, often entertaining owners with their silly antics. Despite their small size, Mini Bull Terriers need firm training and consistent boundaries as they can be stubborn and boisterous.

conditions_detail.name: Lens Luxation
conditions_detail.description: Lens luxation is displacement of the lens from its normal position due to breakdown of the suspensory ligaments (zonules). Primary lens luxation is hereditary. A luxated lens can cause acute glaucoma and retinal detachment.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Surgical removal of the luxated lens (intracapsular extraction) is the treatment of choice. Medical management with miotics helps when surgery is not immediately possible. Glaucoma treatment may also be required.
prevention_tips: DNA test available (ADAMTS17 mutation) — do not breed affected dogs

conditions_detail.name: Patellar Luxation
conditions_detail.description: Patellar luxation occurs when the kneecap slips out of its normal groove in the femur. It can be medial (inward) or lateral (outward) and ranges from grade 1 (occasional slipping) to grade 4 (permanent dislocation).
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Grade 1-2 cases are managed conservatively with weight control and physiotherapy. Grade 3-4 typically require surgical correction to deepen the patellar groove and realign the tibial crest.
prevention_tips: Breed from dogs with normal patellar scores
Avoid high-impact activities in affected puppies
